anchors.title: Reasoning beats confident tone
anchors.decision_rule: Prefer the argument with a clearer claim, better reasons, and evidence that is explained, even if the other paper sounds more polished.

anchors.title: Unsupported persuasion is thin
anchors.decision_rule: A fluent paper with broad opinion statements should lose to a rougher paper that gives specific support and connects it to the claim.

anchors.title: Counterargument matters only when assigned
anchors.decision_rule: Treat counterargument handling as decisive when the prompt asks for it; otherwise use it as supporting evidence of argumentative control.

caution_checks: Which essay proves the claim rather than merely repeating it?
Are examples specific enough to support the argument?
Is tone being overvalued against stronger reasoning?
